questions.question: The Harappan site Kalibanga is located in which among the following Indian states?
questions.explanation: Kalibanga is located on the banks of the river Ghaggar in Hanumangarh district in Rajasthan. It was excavated by A Ghosh in 1951 followed by BV Lal and BK Thapar (1961). It had ploughed field surface, 7 fire altars, decorated bricks, wheels of a toy cart etc when it was found

questions.question: Who among the following is considered as the founder of the Sankhya Darshana?
questions.explanation: Various Indian schools of philosophy and their founders: • Sankhya Darshana: Kapila • Yoga Darshana: Patanjali • Nyaya Darshana: Akshapada Gautama • Vaisheshika Darshana: Uluka Kanada • Mimansa: Jaimini • Vedant: Badarayana

questions.question: Who among the following was considered as the most important God during the Vedic era?
questions.explanation: Indra, Varuna, Agni were the most popular deities for the Vedic Aryans. Indra or Purandara was the most important God of the period as around 250 Rigvedic hymns are dedicated to him. He played the role of warlord and was considered to be the Rain God

questions.question: Who among the following was known by the title of Kunika?
questions.explanation: Ajatashatru was the son of Bimbisara and was also known as Kunika as he ruled from 492 BC to 460 BC. He followed a more aggressive policy than his father and gained complete control of Kashi and broke the earlier amicable relations by attacking his maternal uncle Prasenjit, the King of Kosala. He built the fort of Rajgriha

questions.question: Gautama Buddha passed away at the age of-
questions.explanation: At the age of 80 years, Gautama Buddha passed away in 483 BC at Kushinagar in UP. This incident is known as Mahaparinirbana in Buddhist ideology. The village where he breathed his last is similar to the village Kasia in Kushinagar district in UP

questions.question: Mahavibhasa Shastra is authored by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: Some of the Sanskrit Texts in Buddhism and their authors: • Buddha Charita, Saundarananda, Sutralankar, Sariputra Prakaran and Vajra Suchi: Ashwaghosa • Visuddhamagga, Athakathayen and Sumangalvasini: Buddhaghosa • Madhyamika Karika and Prajnaparamita Karika: Nagarjuna

questions.question: Vallabhi University was established by Maitrak ruler Bhatatrak in-
questions.explanation: Some of the Buddhist Universities along with their locations: • Nalanda: Badagaon, Bihar • Odantapuri: Biharsharif, Bihar • Vikramshila: Bhagalpur, Bihar • Somapuri: North Bengal • Jagadal: Bengal • Vallabhi: Gujarat

questions.question: Mahavira is considered as the .... Tirthankar in the Jain Tradition
questions.explanation: According to Jain Tradition, there were 24 Tirthankars (literally Ford maker, across the stream of existence), the first being Adinatha or Rishabhadeva and the last being Mahavira. The Vishnu Purana describes Rishabhadeva as an incarnation of Narayana. However, historicity of 22 tirthankars is ambiguous and historical proof of only Parshwanath (23rd) and Mahavira can be found

questions.question: The first Jain Council was held in the year-
questions.explanation: The first Jain Council was held in 300 BC in Pataliputra and Chandragupta Maurya was the patron behind it. This saw the compilation of 12 Angas. In the second Jain Council that was held in 512 AD in Vallabhi, final compilation of 12 angas and 12 Upangas took place under the chairmanship of Devardhi Kshmarsramana

questions.question: Who among the following was known as the chief treasury officer during the Mauryan administration?
questions.explanation: Some important officials during the Mauryan rule: • Samaharta: The collector general of revenue • Dharmastha: Chief Justice of civil court • Pradeshta: Chief Justice of criminal court • Dhamma Mahamatra: A new post created by Ashoka empowered with the dual functions of propagating dhamma as well as taking care of the folks for material well being • Rashtrapala: The viceroys in charge of a province • Pradesika: They were the modern district magistrate

questions.question: Bharhut Stupa which is a famous monument in India was built during the reign of-
questions.explanation: During the Sunga rule from 185 BC - 73 BC, there was a revival of Bramhanical traditions and the Bhagavata religion became the most important. Patanjali, the author of Mahavashya was born in Gonarda in Central India during this time as he was the priest in 2 Ashwamedha Yajnas. The fine gateway railing that surrounds the Sachi Stupa was built during this period along with the famous Bharhuta Stupa

questions.question: Pulamavi III was the last ruler of the Satvahanas. He was the ... ruler of the dynasty
questions.explanation: Simuka was the founder of the Satvahana dysnasty in the western part of India as the capital was in Pratisthan or Paithan in Maharashtra. They were considered identical with the Andhras who were mentioned in the Puranas. Gautamiputra Satkarni was the greatest ruler of the dynasty who ruled from 106 AD to 130 AD whereas Pulamavi III was the last ruler

questions.question: Hatigumpha inscription is related to Kharvela of Kalinga. He belonged to which among the following dynasties?
questions.explanation: After the defeat in the hands of Ashoka, Chedi dynasty rose in Kalinga in the 1st century BC and the information regarding the same is derived from the Hatigumpha inscription related to Kharvela, who was the 3rd ruler of the dynasty. He was a follower of Jainism

questions.question: Who among the following is also known as 'Second Ashoka'?
questions.explanation: Kushans were basically from Central Asia and the greatest ruler in the dynasty was Kanishka who ruled from 78-101 AD and he was known as 'Second Ashoka'. He started an era in 78 AD which is now known as Saka era and is also followed by the Government of India. The capitals of the dynasty were Peshawar and Mathura. Kanishka was a great patron of Buddhism

questions.question: Who among the following was known as 'Sakari' among the ancient Indian kings?
questions.explanation: Chandrgupta II was one of the famous Gupta rulers after Samudragupta and he ascended the throne after Ramgupta who ruled for a very short tenure. He extended the limits of his empire by matrimonial alliances as well as conquests as in Western India. Due to his conquests in West India, he ended the Saka rule there and assumed the title of 'Sakari'

questions.question: Hiuen Tsang visited India during the rule of whom among the following?
questions.explanation: Harshavardhan maintained diplomatic ties with China and sent missionaries to China in 641 AD as a result of which, three subsequent Chinese missions visited his court. Hieun Tsang, the celebrated Chinese pilgrim came to his court and spent about 8 years in the dominions of Harsha

questions.question: Dantidurg is known as the founder of which among the following dynasties?
questions.explanation: Dantidurg (752-756 AD) who fixed his capital at Malkhed in Gulbarga district in Karnataka founded the Rashtrakuta kingdom. The greatest rulers of this dynasty were Govinda III and Amoghavarsha. The famous rock cut temple of Kailash at Ellora was built by one of the Rashtrakuta kings named Krishna I

questions.question: The first Muslim invasion in India happened in the year-
questions.explanation: The first Muslim invasion in Delhi happened during 712 AD when Mohammed Bin Qasim invaded India and conquered Sindh which came under the kingdom of Omayyad Khilafat. After this, Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni invaded India 17 times and destroyed the Somnath temple in 1026 AD in Gujarat

questions.question: Adhai din ka Jhopra is considered as a famous mosque built during the Sultanate period. It is located in-
questions.explanation: Qutubuddin Aibak is considered as the founder of the Slave dynasty in India which started in 1206 AD. He dies in 1210 AD playing polo. He constructed two famous mosques namely the Quwat ul Islam in Delhi and Adhai din ka Jhopra in Ajmer. He also began the construction of Qutub Minar in the memory of Sufi saint Khwaja Qutubuddin Bakhtiyar Qafi

questions.question: Who among the following is known as Andhra Bhoja or Andhra Pitamaha?
questions.explanation: Krisha Deva Raya ascended the throne of Vijaynagar in 1509 AD and ruled till 1529 AD. He maintained friendly relations with the Portugese and was instrumental in building temples such as Vitthal Swami temple and Vijaya Mahal. He took the titles of Yavanaraja Sthapnacharya and Abhinava Bhoja. He was also known as Andhra Bhoja

questions.question: Who is known as the founder of the city of Hyderabad?
questions.explanation: Mohammad Quli Qutub Shah was the greatest ruler of the Qutub Shahi dynasty and it was he who founded the city of Hyderabad which was actually known as Bhagyanagar after the name of the Sultan's favourite, Bhagyamati. He also built the famous Charminar

questions.question: 'Bhavarthapadika' was authored by whom among the following?
questions.explanation: The fountain-head of the Bhkati movement in Maharashtra and the founder of the Marathi language and literature, Jnanadeva, wrote a long commentary on the Bhagvad Gita, called the 'Bhavarthapadika', more commonly known as 'Jnaneshvari'

questions.question: The real name of Nurjahan was-
questions.explanation: In 1611, Jahangir married Mihar-un-Nisa, the widow of Sher Afghan, a Persian nobleman of Bengal. Later on she was given the title of Nurjahan. Nurjahan exercised tremendous rights over the state affairs as she was made the official Padshah Begum

questions.question: Which among the following was the official language of the Mughal Empire?
questions.explanation: It was during the reign of Akbar that Mughal empire came to its real form as he was instrumental in extending as well as stabilizing the kingdom. He was the first Mughal ruler who divorced religion from politics and also made Persian the one and only official language of the Mughal empire. He was known as the real founder of the Mughal empire

questions.question: Who among the following was known as the 'Raj Kavi' during the Mughal rule?
questions.explanation: Some of the titles given by various Mughal rulers: • Jagat Guru: Harivijay Suri • Zari Kalam: Mohammad Hussain • Sirin Kalam: Abdus Samad • Kavi Priya: Birbal • Nadir-ul-Asra: Ustad Mansur • Nadir-ur-Zaman: Abdul Hassan

questions.question: Among the council of ministers of Shivaji, who was known as the foreign minister?
questions.explanation: Shivaji's Ashtapradhan or the Council of Ministers: • Peshwa: Finance and General Administration, later he became PM and assumed greater importance • Majumdar: Revenue and Finance Minister • Waqenavis: Home Minister • Dabir: Foreign Minister • Surnavis: Head of Royal Correspondence • Pandit Rao: Head of religious affairs • Sar-i-Naubat: Military commander • Nyayadhish: Justice

questions.question: The Convention of Wadgaon is related to which among the following?
questions.explanation: The First Anglo Maratha War took place during 1775 to 1782 AD. Favouring the cause of Raghoba or Raghunath Rao for Peshwaship, English came in conflict with the Marathas. On being defeated, the British had to sign the humiliating Convention of Wadgaon and later on, they signed Treaty of Salbai in 1782 renouncing the cause of Raghoba

questions.question: The first Dutch factory in India came up in-
questions.explanation: The Dutch East India Company was created in 1602 as "United East India Company" and its first permanent trading post was in Indonesia. In India, they established the first factory in Masulipattanam in 1605, followed by Pulicat in 1610, Surat in 1616, Bimilipatam in 1641 and Chinsura in 1653.

questions.question: Who was the Viceroy of India when Ryotwari system was introduced in Bombay as a revenue collection system?
questions.explanation: Ryotwari system was introduced in Bombay and it was recommended by Lord Munro and Charles Reed. In this, there was a direct settlement between the government and the ryot i.e. the cultivator. The revenue was fixed for a period not exceeding thirty years, on the basis of soil and the quality of crops grown in it. It was based on the scientific rent theory of Ricardo

questions.question: The founder of the Deccan Education Society was-
questions.explanation: Some socio-religious movements in 19th-20th centuries: • Atmiya Sabha: Raja Rammohan Roy • Bramho Samaj: Raja Rammohan Roy • Dharma Sabha: Radhakant Dev • Tattavodhini Sabha: Devendranath Tagore • Nirankaris: Dayal Das, Darbara Singh, Rattan Chand etc • Manav Dharma Sabha: Durgaram Mancharam • Paramhansa Mandli: Dadoba Pandurang • Namdharis: Ram Singh • Radha Swami Satsang: Tulsi Ram • Prarthana Samaj: Atmaram Pandurang
